title: X-tra Aerated Vietnamese Chicken Pho
description: A winey, aerated take on traditional Vietnamese Chicken Pho featuring Grana Padano and a dash of nostalgia.
instructions: Combine chicken breast, fish sauce, and 1 tablespoon sugar in a bowl. Let marinate for 2 hours.
Dehydrate the ginger and onion in a low oven (150°F) for 1 hour.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the dehydrated ginger and onion, and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the fish stock, star anise, and cinnamon stick to the pot.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the marinated chicken to the pot, and cook until cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Add the egg noodles to the pot, and cook until tender, about 2 minutes.
Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon sugar and grated Grana Padano.
Season with black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with green onions and a sprinkle of Grana Padano.
Strain the broth through a fine mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Serve the broth over the egg noodles, and garnish with the chicken and green onions.
